commands.repair_reason: Protected maintainer label, missing real behavior proof, and a security-sensitive command authorization finding require human review before repair or merge automation.; Needs attention: The diff adds a persistent config mutation command without an owner/admin gate; no new dependency or workflow supply-chain change was found.; Review finding: [P1] Gate plugin config writes to owners/admins — extensions/codex/src/comm... (sha=4bae6153ae8e57d27076bf6069c5774af2c02a72)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `/codex plugins list|enable|disable` command handling, config-file mutation wiring for Codex sub-plugin entries, help text, and unit coverage.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings from source inspection: an authorized non-owner can reach the new mutating handler because `/codex` only requires auth and the handler ignores `ctx.senderIsOwner`, and the effective policy stays disabled when global `codexPlugins.enabled` is false. I did not run a live command because this was a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Protected maintainer label, missing real behavior proof, and a security-sensitive command authorization finding require human review before repair or merge automation.

target.title: feat(codex): add plugin list enable disable commands
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds `/codex plugins list` to show configured Codex sub-plugins from `plugins.entries.codex.config.codexPlugins.plugins`.
- Adds `/codex plugins enable <name>` and `/codex plugins disable <name>` to update the enabled flag in `openclaw.json`.
- Keeps the command surface scoped to `list`, `enable`, and `disable`; unsupported subcommands report help without advertising add/remove/toggle/menu.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/command-plugins-management.test.ts extensions/codex/src/commands.test.ts`
- `npx oxfmt --check extensions/codex/index.ts extensions/codex/src/command-formatters.ts extensions/codex/src/command-handlers.ts extensions/codex/src/command-plugins-management.ts extensions/codex/src/command-plugins-management.test.ts extensions/codex/src/commands.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `node --check extensions/codex/index.ts && node --check extensions/codex/src/command-formatters.ts && node --check extensions/codex/src/command-handlers.ts && node --check extensions/codex/src/command-plugins-management.ts && node --check extensions/codex/src/command-plugins-management.test.ts && node --check extensions/codex/src/commands.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `/codex plugins list`, `/codex plugins enable <name>`, and `/codex plugins disable <name>` work through the Codex command surface and mutate the configured Codex sub-plugin policy.
Real environment tested: Local linked OpenClaw worktree on macOS, branch `codex/plugins-list-enable-disable`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/command-plugins-management.test.ts extensions/codex/src/commands.test.ts`
Evidence after fix: Vitest reported `Test Files 2 passed (2)` and `Tests 94 passed (94)`.
Observed result after fix: list shows ON/OFF configured plugins; disable flips `enabled` to false; enable flips it back to true; unsupported add/remove/toggle/menu are not advertised.
What was not tested: A live Telegram button flow or live app-server session with a real installed plugin was not rerun in this PR branch.
target.author: kevinslin
